fresh_facts.claim: Blasts were heard in Tehran on April 4, 2026 at 05:00 IST (00:30 UTC), following Iran's rejection of a U.S. 48-hour ceasefire proposal.
Iran rejected a U.S. ceasefire proposal delivered through a friendly nation and responded with continued heavy attacks.
The Strait of Hormuz has been closed to normal traffic since late February 2026 following U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran.
Bahrain sponsored a UN Security Council resolution authorizing 'all defensive means necessary' for six months to protect Strait of Hormuz shipping, with a vote expected the week of April 4, 2026.
Britain hosted a meeting of over 40 countries to form a coalition to secure passage through the Strait of Hormuz.
U.S. President Trump pursued a 15-point peace proposal delivered through Pakistan covering Strait of Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Trump postponed energy strikes on Iran for five days ending around March 31-April 1, 2026, citing productive indirect talks involving U.S. envoys.
U.S. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed on February 28, 2026 in U.S.-Israel strikes, and his son Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed successor by the Assembly of Experts.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, an ex-IRGC deputy and hardliner, replaced killed Ali Larijani as Supreme National Security Council chief following recent political changes.
The IRGC has taken de facto government control amid power struggles within Iran's leadership following the death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.
Iranian hardliners attacked ex-Foreign Minister Zarif as a 'traitor' for urging war's end and called for his arrest and that of former President Rouhani.
Iranian inflation surged with consumer prices at highs and medicine shortages reported, including insulin at approximately $46.7.
Dubai arrested dozens of IRGC-linked money changers, crippling Iranian sanctions evasion operations.
Trump threatened to seize Kharg Island oil and bomb Iranian infrastructure to the 'Stone Age,' with 2,000+ additional U.S. Marines arriving by late March 2026.
A detainee named Amirhossein Hatami was executed by Iranian authorities on approximately March 30-April 3, 2026 for a Basij base fire during January protests.
Recent Iranian protests are described as the regime's most serious unrest, with child recruits dying and the regime prioritizing military survival over civilian protection.
Israel killed an IRGC naval commander and accelerated Tehran strikes ahead of potential war end in late March 2026.
Houthis, described as an Iran proxy, entered the U.S.-Israel-Iran war on April 2, 2026, posing risks to the Bab al-Mandab strait.
Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey relayed diplomatic messages between the U.S. and Iran regarding ceasefire and peace negotiations.
Iran's new leader Mojtaba Khamenei and General Shekarchi issued warnings targeting adversaries globally via Iranian media.
Iranian officials and state media reiterated Iran will not engage in talks without concessions from the United States.
The UN Security Council resolution on Strait of Hormuz shipping protection requires 9 votes and no permanent member vetoes from the U.S., UK, France, Russia, or China.
China opposes force authorization and Russia seeks dilution in the Bahrain-sponsored UN Security Council resolution on Strait of Hormuz protection.
The UN Security Council vote on the Strait of Hormuz resolution was delayed from Friday to Saturday and then to the following week due to ongoing negotiations.
Approximately 20% of global seaborne oil passes through the Strait of Hormuz, the focus of the Bahrain-sponsored UN resolution.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran rejected U.S. ceasefire proposals and responded with continued attacks including blasts in Tehran on April 4, while the Strait of Hormuz remains closed and international coalition efforts proceed despite Chinese and Russian opposition to UN force authorization. Mojtaba Khamenei's succession consolidates hardliner control amid severe internal fractures, economic collapse, and ongoing military pressure.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Blasts reported in Tehran on April 4, 2026 at 05:00 IST following Iran's rejection of a U.S. 48-hour ceasefire proposal, marking continued escalation despite simultaneous diplomatic engagement.
